title: The Detroit City Code Sec. 50-2-66 defined 'Record of Proceedings' for the matters heard before the BZA on (1) July 29, 2024, (2) October 14, 2024, and (3) November 4, 2024 (Detroit City Clerk)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The Detroit City Code Sec. 50-2-66 defined 'Record of Proceedings' for the matters heard before the BZA on (1) July 29, 2024, (2) October 14, 2024, and (3) November 4, 2024  (which under MCL 125.3602(2) should be filed with the Detroit City Clerk)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

DetroitTaxpayer

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Mar-a-Lago
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records (dated from 01/01/2015 to the time this request is processed):
- Any 911 call audios/transcripts, CAD files, incident reports, or other police reports that originate from, or mention, the following address: 1100 S. Ocean Blvd., Palm Beach, FL 33480 -- colloquially referred to as the Mar-a-Lago Club.

I am a news reporter for WIRED (wired.com), so the requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. Therefore, I request that any fees are waived. But in the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.

I am also asking for my request to be expedited. The documents I am requesting are newsworthy, as President Trump owns and frequently conducts business at this address.

If you need to contact me, you can reach me at caroline_haskins@wired.com, or here. Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,
Caroline Haskins

title: Department of Justice (DOJ) - National Security Division (NSD)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Department of Justice (DOJ) - National Security Division (NSD)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records including internal memos, legal guidance, and policy papers from January 1, 2020, to the present, regarding:

The digital collective and art group "Do Not Research" (DNR) and the legal entity DO NOT RESEARCH INCORPORATED (EIN: 33-3026686).

Joshua Citarella in his capacity as a researcher or principal of these entities.

Any evaluations concerning the categorization of these groups or their associated digital subcultures as related to "Foreign Malign Influence" or "Domestic Violent Extremism" (DVE).

Any "Foreign Agent Registration Act" (FARA) inquiries or assessments concerning the international contributors or funding of "Do Not Research."

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Isabella Fryeich
